varieties.name: 🧬 HYBRID: First-generation (F1) crosses bred for specific traits, may not reproduce true from seed.
Ceylon/True Cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum/zeylanicum)
varieties.origin: Native to Sri Lanka (Ceylon), cultivated since ancient times (pre-2000 BCE)
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 6-15m tall (coppiced to 2-5m in cultivation), glossy dark green leaves 10-18cm long, reddish new growth, aromatic bark and foliage, pale purple berries
varieties.yield_notes: 200-250 kg dry bark/hectare/year after establishment, 2.7% bark oil with 60-75% cinnamaldehyde
varieties.growing_notes: World's finest quality cinnamon, extensively cultivated in Sri Lanka and tropical monsoon climates. humid subtropical climates growers report successful cultivation with 60-90cm annual growth. Requires 4-5 years before first bark harvest. Partial shade beneficial in first 2-3 years.

varieties.name: Alba Grade
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, highest premium grade (top 5-10% of production)
varieties.traits: Pencil-thin quills 6-8mm diameter, pale golden-tan color, ultra-thin bark layers, paper-like texture
varieties.availability: Specialty spice shops, online suppliers, premium pricing ($35-50/kg). Sourced from youngest inner bark of trees in southern Sri Lanka.

varieties.name: C5 Special Grade
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, second-highest quality (Continental category)
varieties.traits: Quills 7-9mm diameter, smooth and slender, light golden-brown
varieties.availability: Popular in European markets, available through Australian importers, moderately premium pricing.

varieties.name: C5 Grade
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, third-highest quality
varieties.traits: Quills 10-12mm diameter, smooth appearance, deep yellow color
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australian specialty stores, good quality-to-price ratio.

varieties.name: C4 Grade
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, Continental category
varieties.traits: Quills 12-14mm diameter, smooth texture, pale yellow color
varieties.availability: Common in Australian markets, economical choice for quality Ceylon cinnamon.

varieties.name: Mexican Grade (M4 & M5)
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka (named for popularity in Mexican cuisine, not origin)
varieties.traits: M4: 18-20mm diameter, reddish-brown/golden brown color, rough texture. M5: Similar appearance, slightly thinner, rough texture, golden-reddish color
varieties.availability: Popular in Humid subtropical Latin/Asian grocery stores, tropical monsoon climates importers. Good for cooking applications.

varieties.name: Hamburg Grade (H1)
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka, thickest grade
varieties.traits: Quills 21-23mm diameter (thumb-sized), golden brown color, coarser bark, single thick layer
varieties.availability: Industrial and grinding use, available from tropical monsoon climates spice exporters in humid subtropical climates. Economical for ground cinnamon production.

varieties.name: Chinese Cassia (Cinnamomum cassia)
varieties.origin: Native to southern China (Guangxi, Guangdong, Yunnan provinces), cultivated since 2700 BCE
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 10-15m tall (coppiced to 2-3m), greyish bark, hard elongated leaves 10-15cm long, reddish when young, yellow flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high yield, sweeter flavor than Indonesian cassia, 1-2% volatile oil with high cinnamaldehyde (75-90%)
varieties.growing_notes: One of the 50 fundamental herbs in Traditional Chinese Medicine. Less expensive than Ceylon cinnamon. Available from Asian plant nurseries in humid subtropical climates. Used extensively in curry recipes and Chinese five-spice.

varieties.name: Indonesian Cinnamon (Cinnamomum burmannii) - Korintje
varieties.origin: Native to Indonesia (Sumatra, Java), especially Kerinci region of West Sumatra, cultivated since 1825
varieties.traits: Small evergreen tree 7m tall, glossy green oval leaves 10cm × 3-4cm, smooth angular branches, red new stems (identifying characteristic), yellow flowers, dark drupe fruits
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, most economical cassia type, accounts for 85% of world cassia production
varieties.growing_notes: Standard cinnamon in US supermarkets. Sweetest, mildest cassia - ideal for baking. Most Americans recognize this flavor from childhood. Available through specialty importers in humid subtropical climates. Jerinci region of Sumatra produces finest quality.

varieties.name: Vietnamese Cinnamon (Cinnamomum loureiroi) - Saigon
varieties.origin: Native to Vietnam (Yên Bái, Thanh Hóa, Quảng Nam provinces), named after botanist João de Loureiro
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree similar to cassia, grows in mountains and forests at low to medium elevations (up to 2,000m)
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, most potent flavor and aroma of all cinnamon varieties, commands premium price among cassia types
varieties.growing_notes: Bold, aggressive flavor - excellent for savory dishes and pho. Considered superior to other cassia types by many. Trade embargo lifted in 1994, now third-largest cinnamon producer globally. Grown on small Vietnamese farms from seedlings.

varieties.name: Malabar Cinnamon (Cinnamomum citriodorum)
varieties.origin: Endemic to central and southwestern Sri Lanka, rare species
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree up to 10m tall, characteristic lemongrass scent, grows in lowland rainforests 300-700m elevation
varieties.yield_notes: Lower commercial yields due to rarity, specialty product

varieties.name: Indian Bay Leaf/Tejpat (Cinnamomum tamala)
varieties.origin: Native to northern India (Assam, Western Himalayas), Bangladesh, Nepal, Myanmar, cultivated for leaves rather than bark
varieties.traits: Tree 10-20m tall, olive-green leaves twice size of Mediterranean bay leaf, 3 prominent veins (vs. 1 vein in bay laurel), clove-like aroma with peppery taste
varieties.yield_notes: First leaf harvest after 10 years, continues for a century. Mature leaves collected October-March
varieties.growing_notes: Also produces inferior bark compared to true cinnamon. Widely used in Moghul cooking and North Indian cuisine. Essential ingredient in biryani. Different from Mediterranean bay laurel despite similar common name.

varieties.name: Navashree (Cinnamomum verum) 🌱
varieties.origin: Released 1996 by Indian Institute of Spices Research (IISR), Calicut, tropical monsoon climates. Seedling selection from Indian collection
varieties.traits: Superior selection with high regeneration capacity (6-7 shoots/year), very young flushes are purple turning green in 7-10 days, excellent bark characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 56 kg/ha in first 4 years, scaling to 250 kg bark/hectare/year at maturity
varieties.availability: Widely distributed by IISR. Available from tropical monsoon climates Agricultural Universities, IISR authorized nurseries, spice development boards in tropical monsoon climates. Over 50,000 plants distributed across tropical monsoon climates, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu.

varieties.name: Nithyashree (Cinnamomum verum) 🌱
varieties.origin: Released 1996 by IISR, Calicut. Stable selection for commercial cultivation
varieties.traits: Good stable selection producing 4-5 shoots per year, consistent growth pattern
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 54 kg/ha in first four harvests, scaling to 250 kg bark/hectare/year
varieties.availability: Widely available from IISR network, tropical monsoon climates spice nurseries. Distributed alongside Navashree in government programs.

varieties.name: Konkan Tej (Cinnamomum verum)
varieties.origin: Released for Konkan region (coastal belt of Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka)
varieties.traits: High regeneration capacity, well-adapted to coastal conditions
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential in coastal regions
varieties.availability: Available from Maharashtra and Karnataka agricultural universities, some tropical monsoon climates nurseries.

varieties.name: Yercaud-1 / YCD-1 (Cinnamomum verum)
varieties.origin: Released for Tamil Nadu cultivation, named after Yercaud hills
varieties.traits: Adapted to mid-elevation conditions (500-1000m), good growth characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Stable yields in Tamil Nadu conditions
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu agricultural universities, available in tropical monsoon climates through spice development programs.

varieties.name: Sugandhini (Cinnamomum verum)
varieties.origin: Indian selection, name means 'fragrant' in Sanskrit
varieties.traits: Notably aromatic bark and leaves, good regeneration
varieties.availability: Available from select tropical monsoon climates nurseries and spice research stations.

varieties.name: PPI-1 (Cinnamomum verum)
varieties.origin: Selection from Indian breeding program
varieties.traits: Consistent performance, good shoot production
varieties.availability: Available through government spice programs in tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: RRL(B) C-6 (Cinnamomum verum)
varieties.origin: Improved variety from Regional Research Laboratory, Bhubaneswar
varieties.traits: Good growth characteristics
varieties.availability: Limited availability, mainly through research institutions.

varieties.name: Randathara Estate Cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum) 🏆
varieties.origin: Auyarakandy near Telicherry, tropical monsoon climates. World's largest cinnamon estate (85 hectares)
varieties.traits: Estate-cultivated Ceylon cinnamon with exceptional quality characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: High commercial yields from estate management
varieties.availability: Commercial estate production exported internationally. Available through tropical monsoon climates spice exporters. Material occasionally available for planting from estate sources.

varieties.name: Kerala Alba Grade
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates Western Ghats, finest young bark selection
varieties.traits: Rare grade (not widely commercially produced in tropical monsoon climates), 3-5 times higher price than H1
varieties.availability: Limited availability, on-demand production, specialty tropical monsoon climates exporters

varieties.name: Kerala H1 (Hamburg Grade 1)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates production, most popular export grade
varieties.traits: 21-23mm diameter, golden-brown color, good smell and sweet taste
varieties.availability: Widely available, best quality and economical. Main tropical monsoon climates commercial grade.

varieties.name: Kerala M4 (Mexican Grade 4)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates traditional production
varieties.traits: 18-20mm diameter, reddish-brown/golden-brown color, rough texture, very similar to M5
varieties.availability: Common in tropical monsoon climates spice markets and local vendors

varieties.name: Kerala M5 (Mexican Grade 5)
varieties.origin: tropical monsoon climates traditional production
varieties.traits: Slightly thinner than M4, rough texture, golden-reddish brown, slightly reddish color
varieties.availability: Widely popular grade in tropical monsoon climates and India, available through local markets, tropical monsoon climates spice vendors

varieties.name: Chinese Cassia - Kerala Cultivation (Cinnamomum cassia)
varieties.origin: Native to China, introduced to tropical monsoon climates for diversification

varieties.name: Indonesian Cassia - Kerala/Indian Markets (Cinnamomum burmannii)
varieties.origin: Native to Indonesia, not traditionally grown in tropical monsoon climates but available as commodity
varieties.availability: Primarily imported dried bark in tropical monsoon climates markets. Live plants rare but theoretically suitable for cultivation.

varieties.name: Vietnamese Cassia - Kerala Import Markets (Cinnamomum loureiroi)
varieties.origin: Native to Vietnam, available as import in tropical monsoon climates spice trade
varieties.availability: Available as dried spice in tropical monsoon climates markets. Not traditionally cultivated in India. Could be introduced experimentally.

varieties.name: Indian Bay Leaf/Tejpat - Kerala Cultivation (Cinnamomum tamala) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to northern India, cultivated throughout tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Tree 10-20m tall, olive-green leaves with 3 prominent veins, strong cassia-cinnamon aroma
varieties.availability: Widely available from tropical monsoon climates nurseries, homesteads, agricultural universities. Used extensively in tropical monsoon climates cuisine. Common in tropical monsoon climates region gardens.

varieties.name: Malabar Cinnamon - Rare Kerala Native (Cinnamomum citriodorum) 🏆
varieties.origin: Endemic to southwestern Sri Lanka, traditional use in Malabar (tropical monsoon climates) coast
